item.content:encoded: That was seriously adorable. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If that plan ever comes, It better have a tiny airplane

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Absolutely adorable gorilla guerrillas. I love it. +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This gives me the warm fuzzlies, and also has some fun fridge logic to play with too. I dig it! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Heh, I used to own almost exact same monkey.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wanna try and contain them with nerf guns XD

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: One note, the description lists addendum 2 as the reason trips are no longer used, but its addendum 3 that has the reason for that. other than that this was great, +1 :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This SCP is super cute, but imagine if they weren't contained… Every plushie they meet will start acting like an annoying kid and causing a lot of mess around it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Maybe…But it could fun though…it could be….

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Absolutely adorable. The last addendum sold me.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Confirming that the reverts I did earlier were rushed and unnecessary, mea culpa. Thanks to updogg for contacting me via PM to clear up the matter!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
To avoid confusion, I'll describe the grammatical changes I made, in order to not confuse moderation, and this will be standard practice for me going forward.

pre-approved > preapproved

- The most debatable change, Zyn okay'd it. After looking into this, I found that both are actually used very frequently; however, neither of them are technically correct, as it should actually just be "approved", and "pre" doesn't alter the word at all. However, "preapproved" is what most dictionaries will show you, so I made a judgement call that it is more correct. Obviously if the author prefers the other one, that's fine by me.

commercially-available > commercially available
partially-opened > partially opened

- Adverbs ending in "ly" are never hyphenated (with a few obscure exceptions, which these ones do not fall under).

trapsetting > trap setting

"Trapsetting" is not a word. You can't make up compound words on a whim.

…Arcade in Cheyenne, Wyoming after… > …Arcade in Cheyenne, Wyoming, after…

- When you name a State, if other parts of a specific location are included (in this case, a town, but any kind of address in general), you always put a comma after the state.

ficticious > fictitious

Simple typo.

Hope that clears this up, sorry to moderation for the confusion, and thanks for quickly responding to PMs and reverting back to the corrections I made.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You have two instances of "Addendum 3092-2." Other than that, this is amazing for a first skip. I'm looking forwards to your future work.

+1

item.title: Re: Audio Adaptation
item.content:encoded: Sorry for the late response, but- this is awesome! You're a great narrator, and I like how you incorporate the corrections into the audio. Thanks so much!

item.title: Audio Adaptation
item.content:encoded:
The adorableness of this SCiP made me smile, so I made an Audio Adaptation of it! I hope you enjoy it!

https://youtu.be/43nVDNuZXTo

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think it's the first skip based on copypasta. Kudos.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, an interview log would be fun. Maybe the article is better without one though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is really adorable, especially the mock tranquilization part and them giving out apology cards. It'd be fun to read more about the ideologies, maybe in an interview log

Anyway, +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I want one

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good points all around! I've added all these fixes, and I'll credit you in the first post. Thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It does refer to them as well, yes. I was considering doing some sort of split-classification for the two, but I don't see that very often these days at least, so I'm not certain it's kosher.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm of the opinion that this should be safe. Yes the -A instances are sentient (as far as we can tell) and are therefore unpredictable, but the claw machine that they come from only works if powered, given money, and operated, correct? Lock it in a room, nothing happens.

Unless the object class also refers to the 23 contained, "alive" ones?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Excerpted sentences and paragraphs below. It's just a few things that that really bothered me:

+ Click for nit-picking
- Wow, rioludoodle has way too much time on her hands

SCP-3092-A instances are cotton-stuffed felt plush gorillas, possessive of no identifying tags or logos…

I feel like "possessive of" should be "possessing".

They are sapient, communicative, capable of sight, hearing, touch, and ambulation of all four limbs.

This is a list of qualities, but you forgot the "and" that should go before "capable" — the secondary list from "sight" through "ambulation" is finished, but the first one isn't.

Paragraph blocks:

SCP-3092-A instances are remarkably proficient… …attempt to breach containment, with limited success. However, thus far, all SCP-3092-A activity appears to have been intended… …buckets or canisters of various liquids placed atop partially-opened doors.

The underlined part is where I think you could end a first paragraph and begin a second. One paragraph about the guerrilla strategies and cooperation, one about the silliness of their tactics.

SCP-3092-A instances can be ripped, torn, or otherwise damaged… …typically after a long period of exaggerated expressions of pain. However, in cases less severe than complete incineration… …is interpreted by them as medical attention.

Same underlining thing here, in the last long paragraph of the description.

  • Also, "guerilla" has more than one R. "Guerrilla" is the correct spelling.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm glad you enjoyed it!

I'd be happy to fix up a few finnicky things with it- what specifically bothered you about it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I thoroughly enjoyed this SCP article! It goes from strange at the beginning to downright adorable at the end.

I gotta say that a couple of awkward sentences and the very long paragraphs in the description bothered me a little, though. If you don't feel up to fixing these, would you mind if I edited?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Part of me wants to see the two team up and concoct some elaborate plan to fly out of the Foundation site. Part of me is scared these guys would pop one by accident and never forgive themselves.

I'm glad you liked it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well done! I loved this every step of the way, you did a fine job characterizing these adorable little things. Reminds me of SCP-2258, they should totally hang out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks! I sort of wanted this to give off that confused, somewhat gushy "these things could be Keter class if they wanted to be, but they don't, so instead they're adorable" vibe.

I'm glad you enjoyed it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"and at least one Foundation agent armed with a net launcher mock tranquilizer rifle"

First thing in, along with the picture, and I'm almost immediately hooked. Most SCPs have containment procedures that of course make you think "Why do we need these specific implements to contain this.", which can stir up a great number of emotions, and this immediately caught my eye as one of the better examples of how to perform this function. This is a great hook, in my opinion. What would necessitate changing from net launchers, which can actually work, to mock tranquilizer rifles?

It was very nice to me how it seemed to be wishy-washy about whether or not it wanted you to be afraid, or at least that's how it seemed to me. Most SCPs start you off with a sense of unease just by the nature of the site and the stuff you're reading, and very rapidly you can adjust - is this a funny skip? a sad skip? a scary skip? I wasn't sure which one of these I was going to get, which was one of the things that made me keep reading, and then when it wrapped up, I let out a nice little "D'aww".

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If your opponent is of choleric temperament, seek to irritate him. - Sun Tzu, The Art of War

What the fuck did you just fucking say about me, you little bitch? I’ll have you know I graduated top of my class in the Navy Seals, and I’ve been involved in numerous secret raids on Al-Quaeda, and I have over 300 confirmed kills. I am trained in gorilla warfare and I’m the top sniper in the entire US armed forces. You are nothing to me but just another target. I will wipe you the fuck out with precision the likes of which has never been seen before on this Earth, mark my fucking words. You think you can get away with saying that shit to me over the Internet? Think again, fucker. As we speak I am contacting my secret network of spies across the USA and your IP is being traced right now so you better prepare for the storm, maggot. The storm that wipes out the pathetic little thing you call your life. You’re fucking dead, kid. I can be anywhere, anytime, and I can kill you in over seven hundred ways, and that’s just with my bare hands. Not only am I extensively trained in unarmed combat, but I have access to the entire arsenal of the United States Marine Corps and I will use it to its full extent to wipe your miserable ass off the face of the continent, you little shit. If only you could have known what unholy retribution your little “clever” comment was about to bring down upon you, maybe you would have held your fucking tongue. But you couldn’t, you didn’t, and now you’re paying the price, you goddamn idiot. I will shit fury all over you and you will drown in it. You’re fucking dead, kiddo. - Navy Seals copypasta

First SCP! Woo! party horn noise

This was a fun little idea I had last night! I think it turned out okay. Many thanks to the following for feedback:

Dr0Shadow (in chat)
Modern_Erasmus (on the forums)
DECEARING EGG (on the forums)
tk4l/Edward Noughts (in chat, some post-mainlist sentence reworking)
rioludoodle (post-mainlist grammar/sentence fixes)

Image taken by me, of one of my stuffed animals from when I was little. Cute little guy.

Hope you enjoyed!
